meta.title: The Epistemic Weight Tensor Simulation
meta.short_tagline: An architectural intervention that dynamically re-weights attention heads during the forward pass to prevent confident hallucinations and causal rung collapse.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: The Epistemic Weight Tensor (EWT) is an advanced architectural module designed to address a critical failure mode in modern large language models: confident hallucination. Operating within architectures like DeepSeek v3 and Kimi K2.x, the EWT dynamically intervenes during the forward pass by bifurcating attention heads into semantic predictors and epistemic validators. By monitoring token-level entropy and evaluating the structural integrity of the reasoning chain via attention subgraph transition matrices, the EWT mitigates 'Causal Rung Collapse' to ensure logical consistency over grammatical complacency.

summary_card.label: Core Mechanism
summary_card.title: Inference-Time Activation Steering
summary_card.body: The EWT intervenes at inference time by artificially boosting the weights of epistemic validator heads when it detects a drop in token-level entropy accompanied by a degradation of logical dependency. This prevents the model from confidently emitting hallucinatory tokens.

key_concepts.title: Key Architectural Concepts

cards.title: Dynamic Head Clustering
cards.body: Attention heads are continuously bifurcated into two clusters. 'Semantic Predictors' exhibit high temporal variance and model fluid syntax. 'Epistemic Validators' exhibit low temporal variance, high spectral entropy, and high activation sparsity, acting as focused fact-checkers.

cards.title: Causal Rung Collapse (CaR)
cards.body: A failure state where the attention mechanism loses structural connection to the reasoning chain established in preceding tokens. It is detected by extracting the attention subgraph of the last 10 tokens and applying Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to calculate the spectral gap.

cards.title: Fluency-Rigor Antagonism
cards.body: The fundamental mathematical trade-off encountered when boosting epistemic heads. Because attention uses softmax, amplifying epistemic heads non-linearly suppresses semantic heads. Over-boosting forces a KL divergence from the syntactic manifold, resulting in text that is logically rigorous but grammatically shattered.

key_concepts.intro: Understanding the theoretical mechanics and mathematical limits behind the Epistemic Weight Tensor simulation.

workflow.title: EWT Operational Pipeline

steps.label: Observe (Dynamic Clustering)
steps.summary: Analyze head activations across a sliding window.
bullets: Compute temporal variance across the rolling activation buffer.
Compute spatial/spectral entropy of attention distributions.
Assign heads to semantic vs. epistemic clusters based on heuristic scoring.

steps.label: Orient (CaR Detection & Entropy Check)
steps.summary: Evaluate the current model state for hallucination precursors.
bullets: Compute Shannon entropy of the next-token distribution to detect high confidence.
Perform SVD on the recent attention subgraph.
Calculate spectral gap to assess the rank and logical integrity of the dependency chain.

steps.label: Decide & Act (Intervention)
steps.summary: Apply the Epistemic Weight Tensor if thresholds are crossed.
bullets: Trigger intervention if entropy is critically low AND logical dependency is degrading.
Apply target EWT multiplier to epistemic heads and dampening to semantic heads.
Smooth the EWT transition using Exponential Moving Average (EMA) to prevent lattice fracture and attention hotspots.

workflow.intro: The step-by-step recursive OODA loop executed during each autoregressive step to ensure structural integrity.

checklist.title: Implementation Checklist

items.label: Model Hook Preparation
bullets: Identify Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) or Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) target layers.
Set up state buffers for rolling head trajectory history (e.g., last 10 tokens).
Ensure access to pre-softmax attention logits.

items.label: Parameter Calibration
bullets: Tune 'entropy_threshold' (e.g., 0.45 bits) based on vocabulary size and baseline model confidence.
Set 'epistemic_boost' factor strictly below 2.5 to avoid absolute grammatical shattering.
Configure EMA alpha (e.g., 0.9) to stabilize inference interventions.

items.label: Safety Nets
bullets: Implement fluency dampening (e.g., 0.85) instead of zeroing semantic heads.
Apply output logit temperature scaling (1.1x) during interventions to safely restore entropy mass.
Monitor False-Positive Rigidity during tasks like coding or pure math generation.

checklist.intro: Actionable starting points for architecting the EWT intervention in custom transformer environments.

metrics.intro: Quantitative signals used to evaluate the EWT's effectiveness, structural stability, and intervention thresholds.

items.name: Token-Level Entropy (Sigma)
items.description: Shannon entropy of the next-token distribution measured in bits.
signals: Sub-0.45 bits indicates high confidence, triggering hallucination checks.

items.name: Logical Integrity Score
items.description: Derived from the spectral gap (S[0] / S[-1]) of the attention subgraph transition matrix via SVD.
signals: Scores below 0.5 indicate significant Causal Rung Collapse.

items.name: Epistemic Divergence
items.description: Measures the KL divergence between the intervened latent state and the model's pre-trained syntactic manifold.
signals: High divergence maps to Grammatical Shattering.

metrics.title: Success & Integrity Metrics

personas.intro: Roles relevant to the research, implementation, and tuning of the EWT architecture.

cells: Designs the clustering heuristics and CaR detection mathematical limits.
Extracts SVD transition matrices and tunes the EMA smoothing parameters.
Balances the epistemic boost factor to prevent grammatical shattering.

personas.title: Stakeholders

failure_modes.name: Grammatical Shattering
failure_modes.description: Over-boosting epistemic heads non-linearly suppresses semantic predictors, causing the model to output logically rigorous but syntactically fragmented text devoid of connective grammar.
mitigations: Use a dampening factor (0.85) rather than zeroing semantic heads.
Apply mild temperature scaling (1.1x) to intervened output logits.

failure_modes.name: Non-Linear Logit Fragility
failure_modes.description: Adding differential bias to attention logits breaks shift-invariance, creating attention hot-spots that destroy anaphoric reference and pronoun resolution.
mitigations: Use Exponential Moving Average (EMA) to smooth the application of the Epistemic Weight Tensor across tokens.

failure_modes.name: False-Positive Rigidity
failure_modes.description: Over-reliance on cosine similarity or simple entropy thresholds triggers intervention during valid, highly confident generation (e.g., code, mathematical proofs), inducing over-cautious hesitation.
mitigations: Rely on SVD spectral gap (subgraph structural analysis) rather than purely linear geometric proxies like cosine similarity.

resilience.intro: Identified mathematical limits, architectural vulnerabilities, and corresponding mitigations.
resilience.title: Failure Modes & Mitigations
